title: Signal ID
description: DEPRECATED. Use SignalRef for new discovery, activation, and media-buy signal identity surfaces. Legacy universal signal identifier used by older Signals Protocol clients. Uses 'source' as discriminator: 'catalog' for signals from a data provider's published catalog (verifiable), or 'agent' for signals native to a signal source identified by agent_url.

oneOf.description: Data-provider signal - references a signal from a data provider's published adagents.json signals[]. Buyers can verify authorization by checking the data provider's adagents.json.

source.description: Discriminator indicating this signal is from a data provider's published adagents.json signals[]

data_provider_domain.description: Domain of the data provider that owns this signal (e.g., 'pinnacle-data.example'). The signal definition is published at this domain's /.well-known/adagents.json

id.description: Signal identifier within the data provider's catalog (e.g., 'likely_ev_buyers', 'income_100k_plus')

oneOf.description: Agent signal - references a signal native to a signal source identified by agent_url. Not externally verifiable through an upstream catalog; buyer trusts the issuing signal source's claim about the signal.

source.description: Discriminator indicating this signal is native to the signal source identified by agent_url, not from a data provider catalog.

agent_url.description: URL of the signal source that provides this signal (e.g., 'https://signals.example/.well-known/adcp/signals')

id.description: Signal identifier within the agent's signal set (e.g., 'custom_auto_intenders')
